title: lithocarpus copelandii
intro: Lithocarpus copelandii, also known as the Copeland's Lithocarpus, is a species of deciduous tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). This plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in its leaves and bark. The health benefits of Lithocarpus copelandii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat gastrointestinal problems, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Lithocarpus copelandii are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
